data.topic: Always-on Android selective speaker transcription app with cloud transcription, speaker verification, Bluetooth mic support, GPS tagging, and battery-efficient always-on foreground service
data.what: Always-on Android app that continuously records audio, performs speaker verification against enrolled user voice, and sends only their speech for cloud transcription with timestamps and GPS tagging
data.audience: Frontline workers — managers and supervisors in field environments who need automatic records of technical conversations
in_scope: Always-on foreground service surviving Doze, sleep, low battery
Speaker voice enrollment at first launch
On-device speaker verification filtering only enrolled user speech
Cloud speech-to-text with low WER and timestamps
LLM post-processing to fix transcription mistakes
Resilience to other apps and system battery optimization
data.problem: Frontline workers have hundreds of technical conversations daily in extreme environments and cannot remember all details discussed. They need a frictionless, automatic record of everything they said.
data.why: Legal, operational, and safety requirements for field work documentation. Without this, critical technical details are lost, creating liability and operational risk.
data.end_goal: Production-grade demo Android app submitted to Frontier Audio. Kotlin-based, always-on, with working speaker verification, cloud transcription, GPS tagging, and Bluetooth support.
data.complexity_reasoning: Multiple unfamiliar specialized domains (speaker verification/voice biometrics, Android always-on audio services, cloud STT API evaluation, Bluetooth audio routing) with no existing codebase patterns to reference.
